item.description: Stephen McCullagh was sentenced to at least 31 years for murdering his pregnant partner Natalie McNally.

item.title: Europeans have been enduring extreme heat waves without air conditioning. Americans are confused.
item.description: The World Cup — along with a heat wave in Europe — has brought to the forefront a cultural divide between the U.S. and European countries when it comes to air conditioning.

item.title: Russia may import fuel as Ukraine attacks produce shortages
item.description: Resource-rich Russia, traditionally an energy exporter, is holding talks with other countries about possibly importing fuel, the Kremlin said on Tuesday. This could happen if Russia could reach agreements at acceptable prices, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said, according to the Russian state-run news agency TASS.

item.title: Berlin prosecutors to charge Malta-based money-laundering gang
item.description: Seven men from Russia, Ukraine, Latvia and Lithuania are to face charges of laundering more than €60 million ($68 million), using fake companies, cryptocurrencies, false accounts and cash, German prosecutors announced in Berlin on Tuesday. Two men, aged 59 and 54, are alleged to be the ringleaders of the organized crime group that operated from Berlin and Riga from at least May 15, 2020.

item.title: Israeli military says another October 7 attacker killed
item.description: Israeli forces have killed a senior member of Palestinian Islamic Jihad accused of commanding a terrorist cell that participated in the October 7, 2023 attacks, the Israeli military reported on Tuesday. The cell is also reported to have been involved in the abductions that occurred during the attacks.

item.title: Probe ongoing after six killed in shooting at German youth facility
item.description: Forensic experts and investigators were working intensively on Tuesday to determine the circumstances of a shooting at a youth welfare facility in northern Germany the previous day that left six adults dead. Four women and two men died at a residential home for mothers and children in the northern town of Stade, west of Hamburg, on Monday in what police believe was an attack linked to a custody dispute.

item.title: Iranian activist, two-year-old daughter, sent to Evin Prison, human rights group says
item.description: Khanbeigi was ordered to return to the prison, known for mass human rights violations, after being given leave from her sentence to give birth to Lalehzari. Islamic regime authorities have ordered that political activist Rezvaneh Ahmad Khanbeigi and her two-year-old daughter be taken to Evin Prison on Monday to serve the remainder of the mother’s sentence, according to the US-based Human Rights Activists News Agency, the official news wire for Human Rights Activists in Iran (HRAI).

item.title: Aid workers warn of infectious diseases, overwhelmed hospitals after Venezuela quakes
item.description: Aid groups warned Tuesday that Venezuela's fragile healthcare system is being pushed to its limits nearly a week after two powerful earthquakes, with damaged and understaffed hospitals overwhelmed by the injured and deteriorating conditions in the disaster zone causing infectious diseases to spread. The scores of international and domestic teams across Venezuela remain focused on the search for survivors, with the government death toll surpassing 1,700 and new bodies still being hauled out from the rubble. Venezuelan officials say that more than 15,800 people have been affected by the earthquakes — a figure that reflects the official number of displaced people, U.N. refugee agency spokesperson Carlotta Wolf said on Tuesday.

item.title: Brothers' six-year justice fight over mum's death
item.description: Cruise ship passenger Janet Purkess was crushed to death by a speeding lorry, a coroner concludes.

item.title: Thousands take part in day of anti-migrant protests across South Africa
item.description: Police were braced for unrest as the clocked ticked down on a deadline set by anti-migrant groups for illegal immigrants to be deported, or leave.

item.title: South African protesters call for undocumented migrants to leave
item.description: Anti-immigrant protesters march in Durban as the March and March movement calls for government action on undocumented migration amid a police presence.

item.title: 2 Young Brothers and Their Father Found Dead in Apparent Murder-Suicide: ‘Difficult to Understand,’ Police Says
item.description: The trio was found dead on Monday, June 29, following a weekend of police responding to calls concerning the children

item.title: Iranian clerics call for Trump’s assassination
item.description: Iran’s most senior clerics have called for the assassination of Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title: Lithuanian parliament elects new prime minister
item.description: The Lithuanian parliament on Tuesday elected Mindaugas Sinkevičius, head of the country's Social Democratic Party (LSDP), prime minister by a majority of 80 to two, with 28 abstentions. President Gitanas Nausėda last week nominated Sinkevičius, 42, as prime minister to succeed Inga Ruginienė, also of the centre-left LSDP, in the course of a restructuring of the governing coalition.

item.title: Congo bans mass gatherings over Ebola fears... except for the England game
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) has banned mass public gatherings to halt the spread of Ebola, but made an exception for the country’s World Cup clash with England.

item.title: Irish regulator reconsidering TikTok data-transfer ban after court ruling
item.description: Ireland's data watchdog will decide "in the coming period" whether to pursue fresh sanctions against TikTok after a court told it ‌to reconsider an order that the short-video platform suspend data transfers from the ‌European Union to China. Ireland's Data Protection Commission (DPC), TikTok's lead EU privacy regulator, fined the Chinese tech giant €530 million a ​year ago and ordered it to suspend data transfers to China if its processing is not brought into compliance within six months. The Irish High Court paused the suspension order shortly afterwards pending an appeal by TikTok and while the court this month upheld the DPC's finding that ‌TikTok breached EU privacy rules, it ⁠told the regulator to reconsider the corrective measures imposed alongside the fine.

item.title: The smell of death lingers in Venezuela’s ruined streets as overwhelmed hospitals race to save lives
item.description: Overwhelmed hospitals are racing to save lives in the aftermath of Venezuela’s earthquakes as the stench of decay seeps from collapsed buildings throughout Caracas and families hold onto hope of finding loved ones.

item.description: It comes after the Bishop of Northampton is charged with two counts of child rape.

item.description: William Tyndale’s translation, published in 1526, was based on a then-radical idea: Anyone should be able to read the Bible in their own language.

item.title: Austrian police: Man kills ex's partner with axe, then kills himself
item.description: A 27-year-old man is suspected of killing his former partner's new boyfriend with an axe, seriously injuring her and then killing himself by crashing head-on into a truck, police in the southern Austrian state of Carinthia reported on Tuesday. After the initial fatal attack on the 29-year-old man and his 30-year-old former girlfriend, the man climbed into her car and crashed into a truck on a motorway with apparently suicidal intent, the police report said.

item.description: Renewables were the world’s largest source of total energy supply growth last year for the first time outside a recession, according to a new Energy Institute report, with solar accounting for 72% of the total.

item.description: The earthquakes that hit Venezuela 6 days ago may have damaged or destroyed 58,000 buildings, NASA says, as rescuers race the clock to find survivors.

item.description: Amid a tumult of laughter, a head bobs up and goes under the dark waters of Kyiv's Dnipro river on the hottest day of the year. But for Kyiv's beachgoers, there is more to worry about than just the water.

item.description: Great Britain's watchdog agency is challenging Apple and Google for not allowing developers to talk to or pull users away from their app stores.

item.description: Ground robots are spreading across Ukraine's front, taking on logistics and combat roles while becoming part of a new combined-arms system.

item.title: Italian police make arrests after investigative reporter's car bombed
item.description: Italian police on Tuesday announced the arrest of four suspects linked to a bomb attack in October last year on the car of an investigative television journalist. The four, who were arrested in the provinces of Naples and Avellino in Campania in southern Italy, face charges of possessing explosives and damage to property "while using mafia-like methods," the police report said.

item.title: Germany's Deutsche Bahn ordered to ease rail access for competitors
item.description: Germany's Deutsche Bahn rail operator must make at least 25% of capacity on heavily used long-distance rail routes available for competitors, following a decision by the Federal Network Agency published on Tuesday. According to the decision, DB InfraGo, the subsidiary responsible for rail infrastructure, must in future allocate between 25% and 40% of capacity on such routes to companies other than Deutsche Bahn's long-distance division.

Four people ‌who ⁠were earlier reported trapped had been located ⁠and were safe, but the operation continued, the ​service said.

item.description: By Crispian Balmer ROME, June 30 (Reuters) - One of the most important surviving pieces of ancient Etruscan artwork went on permanent display at Rome's National Etruscan Museum of Villa Giulia on

item.title: Pope promotes Italian nun to top migrant role in his first major appointment of a woman to Holy See
item.description: Pope Leo XIV on Tuesday made his first major appointment of a woman to the Holy See hierarchy, promoting Italian Sister Alessandra Smerilli to head the Vatican office responsible for migrants, the environment and development. Smerilli, an economist, is currently the No. 2 in the Dicastery for Promoting Integral Human Development. As prefect, she replaces the retiring Canadian Cardinal Michael Czerny, who turns 80 this month.

item.title: Man denies murder of Scottish woman on Greek island in 2009
item.description: Jean Hanlon was found dead in the water off the coast of Heraklion 17 years ago.

item.title: Wildfire destroyed more German forest last year than usual
item.description: Germany lost a total of 2,626 hectares of forest to wildfire last year, more than three times the long-term average, the Federal Office for Agriculture and Food reported on Tuesday. The office put the average between 1991 and 2024 at 844 hectares lost to wildfire each year.

item.title: Italy displays paintings from an ancient Etruscan tomb, its latest cultural acquisition
item.description: Italy on Tuesday put on display one of the best known examples of Etruscan painting, from a tomb that has become the latest acquisition in the Culture Ministry’s buying spree of big-ticket pieces of the country's cultural heritage. The ministry announced in May that it had acquired the fresco panels, dating from the 4th century, from members of the Torlonia family, one of Italy’s ancient noble families whose vast collection of antiquity has long been kept out of the public domain. The Francois Tomb was discovered in 1857 by the French archaeologist Alessandro Francois in Vulci, on land owned by the Torlonia family.

item.title: Manhunt launched after Monaco blast targets Ukrainian-born oligarch
item.description: By Manon Cruz, Juliette Jabkhiro and John Irish MONACO, June 30 (Reuters) - Police in Monaco and France were searching for a suspected bomber on Tuesday after three people were wounded by an explosion

item.title: Ukrainian businessman, family seriously injured in Monaco bomb blast
item.description: Authorities said Vadym Yermolaiev was the likely target of a mail bomb that exploded at his home in Monaco injuring him and his wife and son.

item.title: Pope Leo in final appeal to archconservatives not to appoint bishops
item.description: Pope Leo XIV issued an urgent and final appeal on Tuesday to the archconservative Priestly Society of Saint Pius X not to proceed with the appointment of bishops without papal approval. The society, also known as the Lefebvrists, is set to consecrate four new bishops in Écône in Switzerland on Wednesday.

item.title: Ukraine hits Moscow satellite center in large overnight drone attack
item.description: Russia claimed to have downed at least 419 Ukrainian drones overnight.

item.title: What is the Indus Water Treaty and why are India and Pakistan willing to ‘go to war’ over it?
item.description: Future of river-sharing treaty that has survived wars, border conflicts and prolonged diplomatic freezes in the past is now under threat

item.title: Charges for hate crimes in Scotland soar to record high
item.description: A total of 7,115 charges with at least one element of hate crime were recorded in 2025-26.

item.title: A Skydiving Plane Crashed Just After Takeoff, Tragically Killing All 11 People Aboard
item.description: All 11 people aboard a skydiving plane were killed when it crashed shortly after takeoff in northeastern France on Sunday. The aircraft went down around 11 a.m. local time near Tomblaine, on the outskirts of ... Read more

item.title: Gojek co-founder sentenced to 10 years in Indonesia over school laptops corruption case
item.description: Indonesia's anti-graft court on Tuesday sentenced one of the co-founders of ride-hailing and payments giant Gojek to 10 years in prison after finding him guilty in a high-profile corruption case stemming from his time as education minister. Nadiem Anwar Makarim was convicted of pushing his ministry to buy Google Chromebook laptops for schools during the COVID-19 pandemic as the American tech giant was considering an investment in Gojek's parent company. A panel of five judges at Jakarta’s Corruption Court ordered Makarim to repay 809 billion rupiah (about $45.2 million) — a figure prosecutors said represented the value to him of Google's investment in PT Aplikasi Karya Anak Bangsa — and imposed a fine of 1 billion rupiah (about $55,870).

item.title: Putin super-yacht sighted off Danish coast
item.description: The Russian luxury yacht Graceful, said to be the personal vessel of Russian President Vladimir Putin, has appeared on radar for the first time in almost four years, Danish public broadcaster DR reported. According to data from Marinetraffic.

item.title: German-Dutch Corps takes command on NATO's eastern flank
item.description: Germany and the Netherlands have taken over command of NATO's land forces stationed in Estonia and Latvia, establishing a new headquarters to bolster deterrence on the alliance's eastern flank. "It shows we are ready to assume greater responsibility for Europe’s security," German Defence Minister Boris Pistorius said at the handover ceremony in the Estonian-Latvian border town of Valga.

item.title: The Nordic Paradox: even the world’s most equal countries are failing to reduce violence against women
item.description: Sweden, Denmark and Finland report much higher levels of gender-based violence than the EU average.

item.title: Grow Ukraine’s Airpower Impact By Integrating Manned/Unmanned Systems
item.description: Airpower's true measure lies in the effects it creates. The integration of uninhabited aircraft with crewed fighters can create a powerful asymmetric advantage.

item.title: Image of Myanmar army's helicopter on fire is AI-generated
item.description: Anti-junta rebels damaged a military chopper in central Myanmar in June, but an image of a helicopter engulfed in flames does not show the attack. Footage of the incident published by Burmese media did not show extensive destruction as depicted in the circulating posts and the image was flagged as made with OpenAI tools.

item.title: Body of suspected bear attack victim found on mountain
item.description: Fatal maulings in the last three months have jumped fivefold compared to last year, according to government data.

item.title: France moves to curb ultra-fast fashion imports
item.description: The revised law introduces a per-item charge on mass-produced clothing, which is set to increase over time.

item.title: Iran fights to keep grip on Hormuz as US, Gulf allies carve new shipping route
item.description: Iran allegedly attacked commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz to counter a new southern corridor developed by the U.S. and Oman, analysts say.

item.title: Founder of Asian super-app Gojek sentenced to years in jail for corruption
item.description: The case against Gojek founder Nadiem Makarim relates to a laptop deal made during his term as Indonesia's education minister.

item.title: Advocacy groups file complaint against Ghana over Trump deportations
item.description: The deportees were sent to Ghana, then to their home countries, despite earlier rulings by US judges that it was unsafe.

item.title: South Africa deploys police for widespread protests by groups opposing illegal immigration
item.description: Thousands of demonstrators gathered in parts of South Africa to rally against illegal immigration on Tuesday, which some protest groups set as a deadline for the departure of all illegal migrants from the country. The Tuesday deadline set by the groups for migrants to leave was not recognized by South Africa's government, which has maintained that only authorities can enforce immigration laws.

item.title: 'Waiting like torture': Turks despair as Schengen visa delays mount
item.description: Outside the EU's visa application centre in an Istanbul shopping mall, a large crowd waits patiently, clutching thick folders and scanning lists of required documents one last time, their anxiety palpable as they await the security guard's call."Even though all my documents were in order, I was anxiously awaiting the result.

item.title: Animal welfare activists in Montenegro worried about horses in scorching European heat
item.description: As as a heat wave that baked western European countries reached the Balkans this week, animal welfare activists in small Montenegro have expressed alarm over the decades-old problem of abandoned animals. Several unattended horses roamed the outskirts of the capital Podgorica in scorching heat without shelter, water or food. “This is only 4 kilometers (2.4 miles) from the city center,” animal protection activist Marta Darmanovic said.

item.title: New Kazakhstan constitution takes effect as political transition gathers pace
item.description: By Felix Light, Mariya Gordeyeva and Tamara Vaal ALMATY/ASTANA, June 30 (Reuters) - Kazakhstan's President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev has promised a fundamental reshaping of the Central Asian nation under

item.title: Switzerland returns 18 looted Benin Bronzes to Nigeria
item.description: Three Swiss museums have returned 18 Benin Bronzes to Nigeria, a new step in the African's country decades-long struggle to repatriate its looted cultural heritage.View on euronews

item.title: Latvian man convicted of spying for Russia
item.description: A court in Latvia has sentenced a man to five years and six months in prison for spying for Russia, prosecutors in Riga said on Tuesday. The Latvian national was found guilty of passing information to Russian military intelligence, the GRU.

item.title: Media shut by Ugandan military says talks underway to reopen outlets
item.description: NAIROBI, June 30 (Reuters) - A leading East African media group whose outlets in Uganda were shut down at the weekend by soldiers is in talks with the military to reopen, with staff still unable to

item.title: Rescuers pull survivors from rubble 6 days after Venezuelan quake
item.description: Almost a week after devastating earthquakes struck Venezuela, survivors are still being found alive by rescuers that have come from abroad to help. Salvadoran rescue workers reached a 44-year-old man trapped under the rubble of a shopping centre in the coastal city of Maiquetía in the early hours of Tuesday, El Salvador's President Nayib Bukele wrote on the platform X.

item.title: Germany detains Romanian man over alleged neo-Nazi coup plot
item.description: German authorities detained a Romanian man on Tuesday, with prosecutors saying he is suspected of trying to set up a far-right terrorist group with the intention of helping to bring down the Romanian state and establish a new state based on National Socialism. Prosecutors believe the man has been working to establish a far-right extremist group since early 2023.

item.title: German intelligence sees extremist numbers rise as far-right AfD grows
item.description: The number of extremists in Germany is increasing, Germany's domestic intelligence agency said on Tuesday, linking the rise primarily to the growing membership of the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD). In its annual report, the agency - formally known as the Federal Office for the Protection of the Constitution (BfV) - estimated that 58,700 people fell into the category of potential right-wing extremists last year, 8,450 more than in the previous year.

item.title: Few Ukrainians approve of US leadership, poll finds
item.description: Just 7% of Ukrainians said they approve of US leadership, according to a Gallup poll taken in April.

item.title: Pope issues last-ditch appeal, begs breakaway traditionalist group to back off bishop consecrations
item.description: Pope Leo XIV on Tuesday begged a breakaway group of traditionalist Catholics to call off its plan to consecrate new bishops without his consent, calling the move a schismatic act and a “sin of extreme gravity.” “I plead with you and ask you with all my heart: please turn back!” Leo wrote in a letter to the Rev. Davide Pagliarani, the superior of the Society of St. Pius X. Leo issued the last-ditch appeal a day before the society plans to consecrate four new bishops at its seminary in Econe, Switzerland.

item.title: South African cities shuttered as anti-migrant protests spark fear
item.description: By Nellie Peyton DURBAN, South Africa, June 30 (Reuters) - Workers stayed home, shops were shut and buses sat idle across South Africa on Tuesday, as demonstrators gathered across the country for anti

item.title: Venezuela earthquake: La Guaira port turned into makeshift morgue
item.description: La Guaira port in Venezuela is turned into a makeshift morgue after twin earthquakes, with forensic teams processing victims and families waiting for news.

item.title: Ebola outbreak could cost Africa up to $3.6 billion, UN says
item.description: GENEVA, June 30 (Reuters) - The United Nations said on Tuesday that an Ebola outbreak could cost Africa up to $3.6 billion and hundreds of thousands of jobs, potentially causing a development crisis.

item.title: German intelligence sees extremist numbers rise as far-right AfD grows
item.description: The number of extremists in Germany is increasing, Germany's domestic intelligence agency said on Tuesday, linking the rise primarily to the growing membership of the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD). In its annual report, the agency - formally known as the Federal Office for the Protection of the Constitution (BfV) - estimated that 58,700 people fell into the category of potential right-wing extremists last year, 8,450 more than in the previous year.

item.title: Man arrested after arson attack on Islamic prayer hall
item.description: It happened at the Al Madinah Hall on Talbot Street at about 15:35 local time on Monday.

item.title: Indonesian posts falsely claim free school meals scheme to be scrapped
item.description: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto's flagship free meals programme has faced a wave of criticism, but there is no truth to claims circulating on social media that the scheme has been scrapped in favour of cash handouts. The programme has been amended to stop operating during school holidays and its budget has been cut, but government officials -- including Prabowo -- have spoken in defence of the scheme since the posts spread.

item.title: Man charged with attempted murder and arson following Belfast disorder
item.description: The 29-year-old was arrested on Monday in connection with a glider bus which was set on fire on 9 June.

item.title: Ebola in the DRC: At least 300 positive cases unaccounted for [Africanews Today]
item.description: Ebola in the DRC: At Least 300 Positive Cases Unaccounted For [Africanews Today]

item.title: Britain sets out a plan for future defense with a focus on drones and a fight over money
item.description: Self-flying fighter jets, uncrewed submarines and drones will be at the center of Britain’s future military under a defense plan being announced Tuesday that reflects a world of conflicts transformed by technology. The Defense Investment Plan has been repeatedly delayed as military leaders and Treasury officials wrangled over the cost of equipping the U.K. military for an increasingly dangerous world. Like other NATO countries, the U.K. is under pressure to increase defense spending to counter a more aggressive Russia and less reliable United States.

item.title: S. Africa deploys police as anti-migrant protests loom
item.description: South African police deployed to head off unrest and protests on Tuesday, the unofficial deadline set by anti-immigrant groups for undocumented foreign nationals to leave that has already pushed thousands to flee.- 'Rolling mass action' - The leader of the anti-immigrant March and March group, Jacinta Ngobese-Zuma, told reporters last week that June 30 would launch "a national march to freedom, a rolling mass action" until all undocumented foreign nationals were deported, "We are not calling for

item.title: Ukraine's war drone pilots must first survive the 'Killhouse'
item.description: As drones radically change how wars are fought, Ukraine is putting would-be pilots through a test of nerves and skill before sending them to fight.

item.title: World Central Kitchen Is Serving Hope In Quake-Hit Venezuela
item.description: In the aftermath of Venezuela's devastating earthquakes, World Central Kitchen is using fresh meals to bring hope, dignity and comfort to people facing unimaginable loss.

item.title: Australian town on high alert after rare saltwater crocodile attack kills dog 660km inland
item.description: Council CEO Peter Bennett says no saltwater crocodile had ever been spotted around Richmond before

item.title: Australian man charged with murder over death of a teenage girl in Thailand's Pattaya
item.description: An Australian man was charged with murder and concealment of a body in connection with the death of a teenage girl in an eastern tourist city in Thailand, police said Tuesday. Police said she was last seen on security footage walking into a condominium with the suspect, Simon Peter Carman, in the popular beach city of Pattaya about 150 kilometers (93 miles) east of Bangkok. Carman was later seen leaving the building alone with a large suitcase and security footage showed him loading the suitcase onto a motorbike and riding into a deserted area along the railway, local police said.

item.title: Indonesia's Makarim, Gojek founder and former minister, found guilty of graft
item.description: JAKARTA, June 30 (Reuters) - An Indonesian court on Tuesday ruled that Nadiem Makarim, co-founder of tech firm Gojek and a former education minister, was guilty of corruption and sentenced him to 10

item.title: China's Communist party grows - but slowly
item.description: The Chinese Communist Party (CCP) has grown by 1. 01 million members, reaching almost 101.

item.title: Monaco parcel bomb blast wounds Ukrainian oligarch
item.description: A parcel bomb blast wounded a Ukrainian oligarch and two others in Monaco on Monday, authorities said, in an unprecedented act that has rocked the super-safe principality.The Monaco government said the "strong explosion" was caused by a "parcel bomb".

item.title: South Africa repatriations top 25,000 ahead of anti-immigrant ultimatum
item.description: South Africa's security forces said Monday that more than 25,000 people have been repatriated in recent weeks ahead of planned anti-immigrant protests, with many others still awaiting departure.Several governments, including Nigeria, Malawi, Ghana, Zimbabwe and Mozambique, have organised voluntary repatriation flights and buses after weeks of protests, looting and attacks targeting foreigners that have left four people dead.

item.title: Maternity care system in England 'not fit for the now and not fit for the future', says review chair
item.description: The review, led by Baroness Valerie Amos, says it is "not set up to deliver consistently safe, high-quality and compassionate care".

item.title: Bird nests of fibre-optic cables show war's impact on Ukraine
item.description: By Daria Smetanko and Anna Voitenko KYIV, June 30 (Reuters) - Woven from fibre-optic cable and grass, a small bird's nest found near the front line of the war in Ukraine shows how the more than four-

item.title: DR Congo says 1,307 Ebola cases confirmed, including 377 deaths
item.description: Outbreak spreads to a fourth province, Haut-Uele, bordering South Sudan and the CAR, according to a media report.

item.title: Posts disparage Indian visitors to China with AI-generated video
item.description: Video purportedly showing Indian visitors to China sitting on the side of the road in Shanghai is AI-generated, contrary to posts claiming it shows an example of tourists being "lawless". The clip contains multiple visual inconsistencies and was flagged as an AI creation by a detection tool.

item.title: Screen time calculator: How much of your life is spent on social media?
item.description: The average user spends roughly two hours, 39 minutes on social media daily, adding up to more than 40 days a year.

item.title: Manhunt underway after bomb explosion wounds three at Monaco residence
item.description: Authorities in the wealthy city-state of Monaco have launched a manhunt that’s stretched across the border into France after several people were wounded in an explosion caused by a bomb that was planted in a residential building.

item.title: He survived 2 natural disasters in Venezuela's La Guaira. Now he vows never to return
item.description: Venezuelan merchant Grian Serrano has survived two of the country's worst natural disasters: the devastating 1999 mudslides that ravaged the coastal state of La Guaira and, 26 years later, two powerful earthquakes that struck the same region. Bruised around his left eye and across much of his body, 46-year-old Serrano is recovering from the ordeal he endured with his son and mother Wednesday. The three were buried beneath rubble and twisted steel when their eight-story apartment building collapsed in the city of Caraballeda in La Guaira, the state hardest hit by the magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 earthquakes.

item.title: Fire at petrochemical plant in eastern India injures at least 20 people
item.description: A fire at a petrochemical plant in eastern India injured at least 20 people, five of them critically, police said. The blaze broke out Tuesday in a naphtha pipeline at a facility operated by Haldia Petrochemicals and quickly spread to nearby homes in West Bengal’s Purba Medinipur district, about 130 kilometers (80 miles) southwest of the state capital, Kolkata. Firefighters used 12 fire engines to bring the blaze under control, while the injured were rescued and taken to nearby hospitals, police said.

item.title: Firefighters hope for miracle earthquake rescue
item.description: Mark Leeson is helping international search and rescue efforts after two earthquakes

item.title: Nearly five years late, Merkel finally gets her Chancellery portrait
item.description: A portrait of former German chancellor Angela Merkel, painted by the largely unknown artist Jérémie Queyras, is being unveiled at the Bode Museum on Berlin's Museum Island, nearly five years after she left office. The painting is due to go on public display at the Bode Museum on Tuesday for three months before moving to the Chancellery later in the year.

item.title: Venezuela’s opposition leader in exile says she will do ‘whatever it takes’ to return to help earthquake victims
item.description: Machado, currently in Panama, accuses Venezuelan government of blocking her attempt to return

item.title: 'Not very Christian': How Danish Gen Z are rethinking confirmation
item.description: Young Danes are increasingly questioning the religious meaning of confirmation, a Christian rite that has become one of the country’s defining coming-of-age rituals, while turning to secular alternatives that keep the milestone without the church.View on euronews

item.title: Camilla becomes Royal Patron of Brontë Birthplace
item.description: The Queen officially opened The Brontë Birthplace in Thornton in May 2025 after a fundraising drive.

item.title: Monaco parcel bomb blast wounds Ukrainian tycoon, suspected attacker flees to France
item.description: A couple and a teenager were wounded in the explosion that struck around 9:00 pm local time in a residential building on a street along the border with France.

item.title: Scotland's papers: Burnham's 'pitch to Scotland' and Caribbean shooting
item.description: A review of the front page stories from the daily newspapers in Scotland.

item.title: Volunteers to 'drop everything' for Venezuela aid
item.description: Cornwall-founded disaster relief charity ShelterBox is joining the earthquake relief effort.

item.title: New Oxford academic centre symbolises UK's big-donor era
item.description: With its grand atrium, 500-seat acoustics-led concert hall and other state-of-the-art spaces, Oxford's Schwarzman Centre is the newest example of an elite UK university showcasing a billionaire's endowment.Its concert hall, a separate 250-seat theatre and other spaces will host annual public cultural programmes, which launched in April.

item.title: Feliks the eagle is back home in Serbia after a kidnap ordeal in the Middle East
item.description: Feliks' journey resembles a Hollywood movie script, with kidnappers, smugglers and clandestine border crossings. The year-old eastern imperial eagle from Serbia, started flying in August and later set off on his first migratory flight toward the Middle East, only to be captured by poachers, sold illegally and retrieved in a daring cross-border mission. While Feliks returned home safely last week, his ordeal highlighted both the widespread practice of profit-driven, illegal animal trade and an unfaltering struggle by animal protection groups to counter it.

item.title: Macron wears infamous sunglasses to meet sultan of Oman
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron wore sunglasses during meetings with the sultan of Oman on Monday because of an eye problem, French media reported, months after the eyewear drew widespread attention during the World Economic Forum in Davos. Macron wore the aviator-style sunglasses while greeting Sultan Haitham bin Tariq outside the Élysée Palace and kept them on during subsequent talks.

item.title: Missing eagle found safe after two weeks in the wild
item.description: Cillian the Chilean blue eagle was missing for 13 days but has now been found by his owners.

item.title: Brian Clouston, landscape reclamation specialist behind Liverpool International Garden Festival
item.description: Brian Clouston, the landscape architect, who has died aged 91, was one of the driving forces behind the 1984 International Garden Festival in his native Liverpool, which transformed some 235 acres of derelict Merseyside dockland into a landscaped park that attracted more than three million visitors.

item.title: Angry Venezuelans accuse government of negligence and apathy
item.description: People in areas devastated by twin earthquakes say they need more support from the government.

item.title: London firefighters join Venezuela rescue effort
item.description: Seven LFB firefighters are working with locals to help rescue earthquake victims in Venezuela.

item.title: PHOTO ESSAY: Two victims on opposite sides of the global scam industry seek to rebuild their lives
item.description: Separated by thousands of miles, Chris Colocousis and Safeer Mohammed Koorimannil inhabited opposite sides of the global cyberscam industry. An AP/“FRONTLINE” investigation has found that American technology is present all along the digital supply chains that connect people like Colocousis and Koorimannil. Most public scrutiny has focused on the social media platforms victims see.

item.title: Methodology for AP/'FRONTLINE' investigation into how US tech is abused for global scams
item.description: The AP/"FRONTLINE" investigation was based on tens of thousands of leaked scam center files, videos and photos; an analysis with C4ADS of misuse of artificial intelligence at scam centers; an examination of more than 200,000 connections made by devices at four scam compounds in Myanmar linked to entities sanctioned by the U.S. government; and interviews with 58 scam victims and three dozen current and former scammers from 19 countries. The Associated Press analyzed a sample of 202,013 connections made by devices at four scam compounds in Myanmar that have been linked to entities sanctioned by the U.S. government. International Justice Mission, an anti-trafficking non-profit, obtained the commercially available ad tech data, which covers several time intervals between February 2025 and January 2026, and shared it with the AP.

item.title: Monaco explosion injures 3, suspect flees to France
item.description: A blast from an explosive device has seriously injured three people at a residential building in Monaco.

item.title: Four days to make victims fall in love: How global scammers use US tech to fleece people
item.description: Online, Safeer Mohammed Koorimannil, who was trafficked to a scam center in Myanmar, impersonated a 28-year-old Singaporean woman named Ella. In just a month, Koorimannil targeted some 50,000 victims from at least 17 countries, according to records he smuggled out to The Associated Press.

item.title: Reports: Ukrainian businessman among three injured in Monaco explosion
item.description: A wealthy Ukrainian property businessman was among three people injured in the explosion at the entrance of a residential building in Monaco, media reports said on Tuesday, as authorities investigated the cause of the blast. Monaco authorities said three people were injured, while local media reported that two of them were in critical condition.

item.title: Hotel with over 100 recently deported Venezuelans collapsed in deadly earthquakes
item.description: A pair of powerful earthquakes devastated parts of Venezuela on June 24.

item.title: Photos show teen girls in Bali performing a sacred dance during a traditional Hindu ceremony
item.description: Ketut Alit Widiantari, along with more than 30 of her female friends, walked barefoot to the temple courtyard in her village, carefully balancing her movements while wearing traditional clothes and headdress made of colorful leaves and flowers. The 14-year-old girl and her peers in Tista village performed the sacred Rejang Kuningan dance on Saturday during the Kuningan holiday at Bali's Puseh temple, which serves as the spiritual center for the Balinese Hindu community in the area. For Balinese Hindus, Kuningan marks the close of the 10-day Galungan festival, a period celebrating the triumph of good over evil.

item.title: Three Americans dead in Venezuela earthquakes, says State Department
item.description: Three Americans have died in the Venezuela earthquakes, according to the State Department, as search-and-rescue and recovery efforts continue in the South American country. A State Department spokesperson confirmed the deaths of three Americans resulting from the earthquakes to NewsNation’s Hannah Brandt on Monday. The department added that its consular team is in contact with…

item.title: 'Parcel bomb' explodes in Monaco residential area, leaving 2 critically injured: reports
item.description: Surveillance video reportedly shows a man leaving a backpack moments before a blast rocked a building Monaco, critically injuring two people Monday night.

item.title: Fears for people deported from US to Venezuela hours before earthquakes hit
item.description: Search for deportees from US continues in the rubble of the hotel they were taken to in Venezuela’s La Guaira city.

item.title: Britain Will Now Fine You $145 For Running The AC In Your Own Car During A 104-Degree Heatwave
item.description: There are bad ideas, there are British bad ideas, and then there is whatever this is. As large parts of England and Wales bake under a record-shattering 104°F (40°C) heatwave — hot enough to topple a national record that has stood since 1976 — authorities across the pond are reportedly reminding drivers that cranking the air conditioning while parked could cost them as much as £110, or about $145. Let that sink in while you sip your iced anything. The crime here is using a feature that came fact

item.title: Venezuelans search more quake ruins as attention turns to humanitarian crisis
item.description: Will the country be able to care for everyone who needs caring for?

item.title: A rare explosion seriously injures 3 in Monaco as the suspect flees into France
item.description: A blast from an explosive device has seriously injured three people at a residential building in Monaco, and the attacker fled to France, local authorities said. French and Ukrainian media reported that a Ukrainian magnate and his family were those injured. Monaco’s leader Prince Albert II called it ‘’an odious act'' and said all the country's services were mobilized to ensure security.

item.title: Viral monkey Punch targeted with laser at Japan zoo, prompting warning over blindness risk
item.description: "Punch survived his enclosure mates but may not survive the human attention."

item.title: At Least 100 Venezuelans Deported by U.S. Are Missing in Earthquake Rubble After Hotel Collapse
item.description: At least 100 people deported from the U.S. hours before deadly earthquakes struck Venezuela are missing beneath rubble amid the ongoing search for survivors.

item.title: Years on, families bury 68 Indigenous victims of Guatemala civil war
item.description: With burning incense, flowers and tears, Indigenous Maya Guatemalans on Monday buried the remains of 68 victims of the country's civil war years after they were unearthed in mass graves."Thanks to God... we can come here to leave a candle or flowers and chat with his remains," 55-year-old Norberta Martin told AFP, saying soldiers had slit her father's throat.

item.title: Fact Check: Video Of 'Laser Beams' Falsely Linked To Venezuela Earthquakes Was Published On TikTok Months Before The Quake
item.description: Does a video show how multiple laser beams scanned the ground in the instant before the Venezuela earthquakes struck? No, that's not true: One clip in the video was published on TikTok on March 18, 2026, months before earthquakes struck...

item.title: Merz expresses shock after six killed in German youth centre shooting
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz expressed his condolences after six adults were shot dead at a youth welfare facility in the northern town of Stade on Monday. "The news from Stade is profoundly shocking," Merz wrote on social media platform X, adding that many people "who wanted to help and protect others" had been killed or injured.

item.title: 'Deliberate' Monaco explosion wounds Ukrainian oligarch
item.description: A blast from an explosive device wounded Ukrainian oligarch Vadym Yermolaiev and two others in Monaco on Monday, authorities said, in a "deliberate" act unprecedented in the principality."This is the first time in history, to my knowledge, that such an act has taken place in the principality," he said.

item.title: Sadness and joy as breakaway Catholic group nears schism
item.description: The Society of Saint Pius X celebrated the ordination of new priests on Monday, just two days before it could could trigger a full schism with Rome by consacrating its own bishops.What we say is no longer understood in Rome," said the society's Bishop Bernard Fellay, addressing the faithful on Monday.

item.title: Indonesia court to announce verdict in high-profile graft case against Gojek founder
item.description: By Stanley Widianto and Gibran Naiyyar Peshimam JAKARTA, June 30 (Reuters) - An Indonesian court is scheduled on Tuesday to announce its verdict in a controversial corruption case against former

item.title: Migrants in South Africa fear violence ahead of June 30 deadline
item.description: An anti-immigrant June 30 deadline not recognised by authorities is fuelling fear among migrants.

item.title: Russian strike kills woman in Kharkiv as both sides report casualties
item.description: A Russian glide bomb attack killed a 23-year-old woman and wounded 12 other people in the Ukrainian city of Kharkiv, regional officials said on Monday evening, while Moscow-installed authorities in occupied eastern Ukraine said Ukrainian drone attacks had killed three civilians. Kharkiv Governor Oleh Syniehubov said a Russian glide bomb struck near the Kharkiv city centre, damaging commercial buildings, businesses and other infrastructure.

item.title: Sixty-six years on: The promise of DRC independence remains unfulfilled
item.description: The DRC’s independence anniversary renews debate over progress, security and state capacity.

item.description: "If it is found to be uninjured after examination, it will be released back into the wild."

item.title: Young man dies after being attacked by crocodile near resort at popular beach destination
item.description: A young man has died after being pulled into the water by a crocodile near a resort in a popular beach destination late last week. Authorities described the incident as a "highly regrettable, unusual and isolated incident," saying it requires immediate and coordinated action by all three levels of government.

item.title: 'I recognized her ring': identifying Venezuela's dead in a makeshift morgue
item.description: At a makeshift morgue in the quake-hit Venezuelan port of La Guaira, forensic experts in blue gowns and caps picked their way through dozens of bodies laid out in bags under the sun.- Identified by a ring - Doctors and forensic experts in the port examined bodies under tarpaulins held up by four poles before issuing death certificates and cremation authorizations.

item.title: Shein Faces Legal Action in Germany Over Toxic Chemicals in Clothing
item.description: German nonprofit Deutsche Umwelthilfe is taking legal action against Shein after tests found hazardous chemicals in most items analyzed, adding to growing EU scrutiny of the e-tail Goliath.

item.description: German President Frank-Walter Steinmeier said he was "deeply shocked" after six people were killed in a shooting at a facility housing mothers and children.

item.title: Three seriously injured in explosion at Monaco residential building
item.description: Three people were seriously injured, two of them critically, in an explosion at the entrance of a residential building in Monaco, Minister of State Christophe Mirmand said late on Monday. The blast occurred around 9 pm (1900 GMT), Mirmand told French broadcaster BFMTV.

item.description: China’s relative insulation from supply and price shocks will “deepen economic dependencies” that Beijing could leverage for geopolitical advantage, a new report argued.

item.description: Khadijah Farrakhan has died at age 90.

item.description: Three people have been injured in a house explosion in Monaco which has been described as a “malicious act”.

item.title: World Bank drops climate finance targets in renewed action plan
item.description: The World Bank on Monday extended its climate change policy framework indefinitely, but dropped its targets for the percentage of financing that must have climate-related impacts, according to a statement.The World Bank will continue to report on net greenhouse gas emissions and on the percentage of its projects that have a climate-change impact, the statement said.

item.title: 12-Foot Crocodile Seized After Killing Tourist at Puerto Vallarta Resort (Video)
item.description: Authorities have caught the crocodile that killed 28-year-old Irving Mauricio, who was mauled to death in Puerto Vallarta while swimming.

item.title: France to illuminate Statue of Liberty for US 250th birthday
item.description: France will stage an elaborate light show at the Statue of Liberty to mark the 250th anniversary of the United States, the French consulate said Monday.France also dispatched its air force acrobatics team to the United States this month to mark the 250th anniversary.

item.description: The man was on the beach at a resort when the attack happened, authorities said.

item.title: Iran rejects French idea for global effort to demine Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran is firmly rejecting international involvement in mine clearance in the Strait of Hormuz, which was proposed by French President Emmanuel Macron, Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi wrote on social media platform X on Monday. Such a deployment would violate the existing framework agreement, he said.

item.title: IDF seals 16-kilometer Hamas tunnel where murdered soldier Hadar Goldin's remains were held
item.description: The IDF completed a three-month operation to seal a vast Hamas tunnel complex near the Philadelphi Corridor with more than 30,000 cubic meters of concrete. IDF soldiers completed a three-month-long operation to seal the underground tunnel in which the remains of Lieutenant Hadar Goldin were held captive by Hamas, the military announced on Monday.

item.title: Some Russian soldiers last just minutes on front lines, military bloggers say
item.description: Once Russian soldiers reach certain parts of the front lines of the war in Ukraine, they can expect to live an average of just 20 to 35 minutes, according to a grim estimate by Russian military bloggers.

item.description: The blast at a residential building was "very likely an attack", Monaco's head of government tells AFP.

item.description: With tens of thousands of people still believed missing, teams listen for signs of life beneath the rubble.

item.description: Zafar Mawani and Guillermo Ortiz were among four individuals found dead in a mass grave earlier this month

item.title: Germany's outgoing anti-Semitism commissioner takes stock
item.description: The Central Council of Jews in Germany has said the situation for Jewish people is relatively better than in other western countries, despite the large number of anti-Semitic incidents. Central Council President Josef Schuster credited the work of the government's commissioner for Jewish life and the fight against anti-Semitism, Felix Klein.

item.description: A mystery vigilante is targeting suspected motorcycle thieves in Jalisco, and authorities confirm the recovery of stolen bikes.

item.title: Venezuela's quake-hit La Guaira port 'operational': US military
item.description: Venezuela's La Guaira port is now "operational" after undergoing repairs and is being used to deliver aid to the earthquake-hit South American country, the US military said on Monday.The port -- one of Venezuela's two main ports -- "is now operational, and the USS Fort Lauderdale is using the port to deliver critically needed supplies and equipment to Venezuela," US Southern Command said in a statement, referring to a US warship dispatched to assist with aid efforts.

item.title: Audio of Toronto Pride parade livestream edited to include fake chant
item.description: Social media users quickly spread a video which appeared to show attendees at the 2026 Toronto Pride parade chanting "we're coming for your children," evoking unfounded conspiracy theories about children being groomed into joining LGBTQ communities. The clip, however, is altered; the footage was lifted from the Canadian Press's livestream of the event which does not include the falsely inserted phrase.

The final verdict and a possible prison sentence are to be announced soon, a court spokesman said, according to the agency.

item.description: The death toll from Venezuela's twin earthquakes has risen to 1,719, National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez said on Monday. This is more than 250 above the 1,450 previously reported.

item.title: China signals concessions to EU on rare earths as trade talks begin
item.description: China has signalled it is willing to make concessions to the European Union in a dispute over export limits on rare earths and permanent magnets, with Chinese Minister of Commerce Wang Wentao assuring the EU that existing limits would not affect EU supply chains. EU Trade Commissioner Maroš Šefčovič made the announcement on Monday in Brussels following talks with Wang.

item.description: Swiss authorities on Monday returned 18 Benin Bronzes to Nigeria in a ceremony at the National Museum in Lagos, the latest addition to the nation's growing collection of repatriated treasures."The artefacts returned today carry a painful history," Elisabeth Baume-Schneider, a Swiss federal councillor, said at the ceremony.

The suspect had an appointment with many of those he is alleged to have killed at the facility in the town of Stade, to the west of Hamburg.

item.title: Berlin narrowly misses 50% target for women in leadership roles

"The 47% is a clear sign of progress towards equality," said Federal Family and Women's Minister Karin Prien.

item.description: US Central Command (CENTCOM) chief Brad Cooper has met Lebanese President Joseph Aoun in Beirut to discuss implementation of the framework agreement reached with Israel in Washington a few days ago, the Lebanese presidency said on Monday. Cooper also met the commander of the Lebanese Armed Forces, Rodolphe Haykal, according to CENTCOM.

item.title: France to ban ads for ultra-fast fashion
item.description: Advertising for ultra-fast fashion platforms such as Shein, Temu and AliExpress is set to be banned in France soon, following a Senate vote in favour of a bill aimed at curbing the environmentally damaging practice. The law provides for an advertising ban at the turn of the year.

item.title: Israeli troops face resistance in southern Syrian town of Abdin
item.description: As Israeli troops and vehicles entered the town of Abdin in southern Syria, residents blocked the roads with rocks, and some young men and boys threw stones to push back the military patrol. Tensions in this part of the country created by a buffer zone occupied by Israeli forces have flared into violence in recent days, leaving residents anxious that more escalation is coming. Residents of Abdin, located near a U.N.-patrolled buffer zone now controlled by Israeli troops, tried to resist against a military incursion Sunday.

item.description: A passer-by spotted a kangaroo in Elmshorn near Hamburg and alerted the police, but the marsupial was too fast and ended up hopping away from authorities, a police spokeswoman said on Monday. The incident happened on Saturday morning.

item.description: The weapons mounted on a specialized natural gas tanker underscore Moscow’s growing fear of attacks on its shipping in the Baltic.

item.description: Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun on Monday told the head of US forces in the Middle East that Beirut intended to assert its sovereignty over the entire country, with the army deployed right up to the Israeli border.Hezbollah drew Lebanon into the Middle East war in March with rocket fire at Israel, triggering Israeli airstrikes and a ground invasion.

item.title: Resident doctors in England vote for UK government's pay offer, ending strikes
item.description: LONDON, June 29 () - Resident doctors in England have voted to accept the government's latest pay and job offer, their ‌union said on Monday, bringing an end to a ‌long-running industrial dispute that has disrupted the National Health Service. The British Medical Association said ​53% of its eligible members had backed the package in a referendum following the suspension of strike action earlier this month.

item.title: Prince William Considering ‘Turning Part of Buckingham Palace’ Into a Hotel for Monarchy’s Future
item.description: Prince William reportedly has new plans for Buckingham Palace. item.title: Paris funeral homes overwhelmed after record heatwave
item.description: France on Monday counted its heat-related deaths, as a funeral business representative said Paris morgues have been overwhelmed by a spike in fatalities during a record-breaking heatwave.French health officials said Sunday there had been around 1,000 more deaths than during the same period in previous months since Wednesday last week, when France was at its hottest since records began.

item.title: Congo bans gatherings in Kinshasa and three provinces over Ebola outbreak
item.description: DAKAR, June 29 (Reuters) - The Democratic Republic of Congo has banned public gatherings in four provinces, including the capital Kinshasa, as the country battles a deadly Ebola outbreak.

item.title: Jaguar captured after roaming residential neighborhoods in Foz do Iguaçu
item.description: A jaguar that roamed residential neighborhoods in the Brazilian city of Foz do Iguaçu over the weekend was safely captured Sunday.

item.title: Senegalese lawmakers pass divisive reform curbing presidential powers
item.description: Members of Senegal's National Assembly on Monday adopted a controversial constitutional amendment that expands their role and reduces presidential powers, but the government said it will be put to a referendum. The constitutional reform comes as political tensions rose between President Bassirou Diomaye Faye and his former prime minister, Ousmane Sonko, who was dismissed and elected as the president of the National Assembly last month.

item.title: Pakistan airstrikes leave homes in ruins in Afghanistan
item.description: Villagers inspect destroyed homes in Afghanistan's Paktia province after overnight airstrikes, as Afghan and Pakistani officials give differing accounts.

item.description: Police said they were working to establish the background to the shooting and what exactly happened.

item.title: Press watchdog reviewing Gaza casualty database over 'journalists' tied to Hamas, PIJ
item.description: A Meir Amit Center study from December found that 60% of individuals who identified as journalists and were killed in Gaza were members of or affiliated with terrorist organizations. The Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ) is conducting a full review of its database of journalists killed during the Israel-Hamas War after Hamas and Palestinian Islamic Jihad (PIJ) published obituaries identifying as combatants individuals previously listed by CPJ as journalists.

item.title: Six killed in shooting at youth care centre in northern Germany
item.description: Six people have been killed in a shooting at a youth welfare facility in the town of Stade to the west of Hamburg, German police said on Monday, with five dying at the scene and a sixth succumbing to their injuries in hospital. One main suspect - the suspected shooter - has been detained, a police spokesman said.

item.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo spreads to fourth province
item.description: A deadly Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has spread to a fourth province, meaning the country's entire northeast -- home to around 15 million people -- is now affected. Haut-Uele, bordering South Sudan and the Central African Republic, is now the fourth affected province.

item.title: Afghan families mourn loved ones as border tensions with Pakistan rise
item.description: Afghan officials say Pakistani attacks killed at least 36 civilians, while Pakistan says its forces killed 29 fighters.

item.title: Iran’s deputy foreign minister in Oman for talks on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Kasem Gharibabadi flew to Oman on Monday where he met with a senior Omani diplomat for a first meeting of a newly established Hormuz Joint Committee, Gharibabadi wrote on X. The Iranian diplomat met with Abdulaziz Al Hinai for discussions on current issues relating to the strait, including future management of the key waterway and the sovereign rights of both coastal states, the X post stated.

item.title: Heat forces yodelers at annual Swiss festival to sing in fountains
item.description: City fountains became impromptu rehearsal spaces this weekend as yodelers at a festival in Basel, Switzerland, squeezed in last-minute practice while cooling off during Europe’s June heat wave. From Friday to Sunday, singers and alphorn players filled the streets and spontaneous bursts of yodeling echoed through restaurants, where diners initially reacted with surprise before joining in. In Petersplatz, in central Basel, seamstresses remained on call throughout the festival to repair the traditional Alpine folk costumes worn by participants in case of emergency.

item.description: A sixth victim of a shooting to the west of Hamburg has died in hospital after five others were fatally shot at a youth care centre, German police reported on Monday. No information was initially available on the age of the sixth victim.

item.title: Paris's Cirque d'Hiver begins renovation to restore its original splendour

item.title: German court pauses Meta privacy breach lawsuit pending EU ruling
item.description: The Hamburg Higher Regional Court has suspended proceedings brought by a major German consumer group against Meta, the parent company of Facebook, it said on Monday. The Federation of German Consumer Organizations (vzbv) and Meta’s Irish subsidiary had previously failed to reach a settlement.

item.title: UN says 28 civilians killed in Pakistani airstrikes in Afghanistan
item.description: At least 28 civilians, including women and children, were killed, and 49 others were injured in Pakistani airstrikes in three Afghan provinces overnight, The United Nations Assistance Mission in Afghanistan (UNAMA) said on Monday. The agency warned that the figures are preliminary "UNAMA reiterates its call for respect for the international humanitarian law principles of precaution, distinction and proportionality to protect civilians from harm," the mission said in a statement.

item.title: PHOTO ESSAY: Sudan's capital eases to life again after 3 years of war
item.description: Khartoum has become a kind of ghost town after three years of war. Children wander through an amusement park in the shadow of some of the Sudanese capital’s most well-known buildings, now reduced to shells after attacks. The ground is littered with bullet casings, shrapnel and unexploded weapons.

item.title: Russia jails veteran who threatened Putin with mutiny

item.title: Rescue workers race against time after Venezuela quakes kill 1,450
item.description: Rescue workers in Venezuela have continued operations for a fifth day after two powerful earthquakes killed more than 1,450 people, with teams from Venezuela and several other countries searching through the night for tens of thousands still believed to be trapped under rubble. "If there are still survivors under the rubble, every second counts for them now," said Simone Walter, emergency relief coordinator at the aid organization Help, on Monday.

item.title: Wife of Louis Farrakhan dead at 90
item.description: Khadijah Farrakhan, the wife of 93-year-old Nation of Islam leader Louis Farrakhan, died at the age of 90 years old. The couple had been married for 72 years.

item.title: DR Congo bans mass gatherings in the capital to prevent spread of Ebola
item.description: Opposition politicians accuse the government of using the outbreak to halt a planned protest.

item.title: Crocodile Had 6 Pairs of Shoes and a Missing Man Inside
item.description: When a man vanished into crocodile-infested floodwaters along the Komati River, a search team made a grim discovery inside a 15-foot predator sunbathing nearby. What they found alongside his remains raised far more questions than it answered.

item.title: Several killed and injured in latest Russian attacks on Ukraine
item.description: At least five people have been killed in a Russian attack on the south-eastern Ukrainian industrial city of Dnipro, while attacks in neighbouring areas caused further casualties, Ukrainian officials said on Monday. At least 28 people were injured in the Dnipro attack, Oleksandr Hanzha, head of the military administration for the Dnipropetrovsk region, wrote on Telegram.

item.title: Five killed in shooting at youth care centre in northern Germany
item.description: Five adults have been killed in a shooting at a youth care facility in the northern German city of Stade, police said on Monday. "There are several people who have been injured, some seriously," a police spokeswoman told dpa.

item.title: Lukashenko courts Moscow and Beijing as Ukraine tensions simmer
item.description: Belarusian leader Alexander Lukashenko has visited Russia and then China following a series of public exchanges with Ukraine, meeting President Xi Jinping in Beijing and Russian President Vladimir Putin at his Valdai residence. Lukashenko told Xi that coming to China was for him "like coming home," Belarusian journalists accompanying the delegation reported on Monday.

item.title: I Survived Europe's Heat Wave Without AC—No Thanks to Regulation (opinion)
item.description: European countries are stubbornly refusing to adapt to warming weather, with deadly results.

item.title: Three killed in Gaza drone strike, including boy
item.description: Three people have been killed in an Israeli drone strike in the Gaza Strip, Palestinian sources said on Monday. The Palestinian news agency WAFA reported that an eight-year-old boy was among the victims.

item.title: Fuel shortages spread to more parts of Russia as Ukrainian attacks bite
item.description: MOSCOW/ROSTOV-ON-DON/SEVASTOPOL, June 29 () - Ukrainian attacks have caused fuel shortages to spread from Russian-annexed Crimea to nearby parts of southern Russia, and even to the capital ‌Moscow, which has previously been spared, residents say. Motorists in nearly all of Russia ‌are limited in the amount of fuel they can now buy, with particularly severe restrictions imposed in all ​of Russian occupied Ukraine, much of southern Russia and Siberia.

item.description: Analysts warn military pressure alone cannot contain a threat from armed groups that has reached Pakistan’s cities.

item.description: The conference began with a memorial service for IDF soldiers who had been killed over the course of the war, including a minute of silence and a recording of Col. Asaf Hamami from October 7, 2023.

item.title: Soccer Player Lucas Trejo's Wife, Kids Killed in Venezuela Earthquakes
item.description: Soccer player Lucas Trejo's wife Yanina and their two kids Aarón and Ainhoa were among those who were killed in the consecutive 7.2-magnitude and 7.5-magnitude earthquakes that rocked Venezuela.

item.title: Stocked ambulance headed to Ukraine after £20k raised
item.description: "We can't take up arms to defend Ukraine, but we can take a stand with our friends."

item.title: German civil servants allowed to work from foreign beaches eight weeks a year
item.description: German civil servants are allowed to work from the beaches of Europe for up to eight weeks a year under what has been called the “Mallorca rule”.

item.title: Five killed in shooting at youth care centre in northern Germany
item.description: Five people were killed and several others injured in a shooting in the northern German city of Stade, police said on Monday. "There are several people who have been injured, some seriously," a police spokeswoman told dpa.

item.title: Chavez-era housing complex in ruins after Venezuela quakes
item.description: A residential complex once touted as part of former strongman leader Hugo Chavez's flagship housing program has been left uninhabitable in Venezuela's earthquake disaster.Built as part of efforts to modernize Venezuela, the buildings now symbolize the country's dire situation after twin quakes on Wednesday left nearly 1,500 people confirmed dead and tens of thousands missing.

item.title: Claims that Mnangagwa told Zimbabweans to walk home from South Africa stem from satire
item.description: With thousands of foreign nationals in South Africa fleeing ahead of an unofficial June 30 deadline targeting migrants, social media posts claim that Zimbabwean President Emmerson Mnangagwa told his citizens to walk back home. But this is false; the claim originated from a satirical Facebook page and includes a doctored image that misrepresents Mnangagwa’s address at a 2025 regional summit in Kenya.

item.title: Kenya-US rare earths deal challenged in court over secrecy
item.description: A multi-billion-dollar rare earth minerals deal between Kenya and the United States was challenged in a Kenyan court Monday, accused of lacking transparency.The Centre for Litigation Trust, a legal advocacy organisation, is suing the Kenyan government in a bid to halt the deal with the United States to mine the area, after media reports that it was nearing completion.

item.title: Russia's Drone Force Needs A Different Kind Of Recruit
item.description: Russia is recruiting female university students for its drone force as Moscow seeks technically skilled operators for an increasingly unmanned battlefield.

item.title: Iraq's new PM signals wider anti-corruption crackdown after raids
item.description: Iraq’s new Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi has vowed to press ahead with an anti-corruption crackdown after several senior officials and lawmakers were arrested in weekend raids in Baghdad's government district. Anyone in possession of public funds must return them to the state, al-Zaidi was quoted as saying by the INA news agency on Monday.

item.title: Police say 5 killed, 2 arrested after shooting at German youth center
item.description: German police say 2 people are in custody and 5 were killed in a rare shooting that took place at a youth center in Stade, near Hamburg.

item.title: Fife man dies after being shot 'multiple times' in Caribbean
item.description: Daniel Vettrino, 37, was killed following the incident on in the island of Canouan in St Vincent and the Grenadines.

item.title: Iraq's new PM signals wider anti-corruption crackdown after raids
item.description: Iraq’s new Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi has vowed to press ahead with an anti-corruption crackdown after several senior officials and lawmakers were arrested in weekend raids in Baghdad's government district. Anyone in possession of public funds must return them to the state, al-Zaidi was quoted as saying by the INA news agency on Monday.

item.description: The incident took place in the northern German city of Stade

item.description: Stefan Sivers, 35, of Aberdeen, is reported to have been fighting against Russian forces in the Kharkiv region.

item.title: Zelenskyy condemns 'horrific attacks' as Russian strikes kill 11 and wound 40 in Ukraine
item.description: Russian missiles and drones killed at least 11 civilians and injured 40 others in Ukraine on Monday in what President Volodymyr Zelenskyy described as “horrific attacks.” Since Russia launched its all-out invasion of its neighbor more than four years ago, its forces have conducted bombing in an effort to destroy Ukraine’s infrastructure and sap morale. A Russian missile targeting infrastructure struck the central city of Dnipro, killing five people and wounding 29, Zelenskyy said on social media.

item.title: Uganda orders closure of two major media outlets
item.description: The head of the country’s military, who is also the son of the current president, said he did not “believe in ‌a free press.”

item.title: Five killed in shooting in northern Germany, police say
item.description: Five people have been killed in a shooting in the northern German city of Stade, police told dpa on Monday, adding that a suspect has been detained. "Shots were fired near a youth centre in the city centre," a spokesman said in response to an enquiry.

item.title: German court jails man for life for 1984 murder of ex-girlfriend
item.description: A German court on Monday sentenced a 67-year-old man to life imprisonment for the murder of his ex-girlfriend more than four decades ago. The defendant confessed to killing the 19-year-old trainee nurse in her room on July 30, 1984.

item.title: A strong aftershock rattles Venezuela as rescue workers race to find survivors
item.description: A strong aftershock jolted Venezuela early Monday following last week’s devastating back-to-back earthquakes, as civilians and emergency responders kept combing through the ruins of fallen buildings for survivors. The aftershock, which struck about 27 kilometers (17 miles) north of Caraballeda on Venezuela’s Caribbean coast at 7:01 a.m. local time, measured 4.6 on the Richter scale, according to the United States Geological Survey. Colombia’s geological survey put the magnitude at 5.1.

item.title: Soccer star's wife and 2 children killed in Venezuela earthquakes, team says
item.description: The wife and two children of Argentine soccer star Lucas Trejo died after powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela, his team said.

item.description: Five people have been killed in a shooting in northern Germany.

item.description: Lessons from the Iran war could influence future UAE military acquisitions, aside from its enduring diversification policy, which this prospective Indian deal reinforces.

item.title: Venezuela earthquake rescues: Five heart-warming moments
item.description: Amid the death and destruction, a few fleeting moments have brought rare relief to Venezuelans.

" Iranian Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi called for a 'Global Week of Action for a Free Iran' coinciding with the Iranian regime's planned burial of former supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, beginning on July 4.

item.description: Power outages have hit Russian-occupied parts of southern Ukraine, according to the Moscow-affiliated regional authorities. "All districts of the Kherson region are fully or partially without electricity," Vladimir Saldo, the head of the region's occupying Russian administration, wrote on Telegram.

item.description: Emergency services were called to the scene on the A714 in Dumfries and Galloway on Saturday night.

item.description: China has placed a further 20 Japanese companies on an export control list, barring them from receiving Chinese goods with both civilian and military applications, in a move set to heighten tensions between the two countries. China's Commerce Ministry said on Monday the measure affects Japan's National Institute for Defence Studies and subsidiaries of Mitsubishi Electric, among others.

item.title: Bondi Beach attack survivor tells of 'trauma' of online AI images
item.description: A Jewish lawyer wounded in a mass shooting at Australia's Bondi Beach discovered that AI-generated images depicting him as a "crisis actor" were proliferating online as he was going into surgery, a royal commission inquiry heard Monday.His story emerged in hearings of the federal royal commission, the highest level of public inquiry, which was formed after the December 14 attack, Australia's deadliest in decades.

item.title: Senior counsel's quote on former Sri Lankan president Mahinda Rajapaksa is fabricated
item.description: Politicians across party lines attended the funeral of prominent Sri Lankan lawyer Upul Kumarapperuma's son, including former president Mahinda Rajapaksa. But a purported quote attributed to Kumarapperuma about the ex-leader's attendance, shared in a news graphic that features the logo of a local news website, is fabricated. The senior lawyer said he did not make those remarks, while the outlet said the graphic is fake.

item.title: Reports: U.S., Iran agree to halt attacks following escalation
item.description: The United States and Iran have agreed to stop attacking one another, a move that could end days of escalating strikes that threatened their truce.

item.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill 36 civilians in Afghanistan and wound 160, officials say
item.description: Overnight ground operations and strikes by Pakistani forces have killed at least 36 civilians and injured more than 160 others, Afghan officials said Monday, as tensions between the neighbors further escalated. Pakistani security forces carried out a ground operation along the Pakistan-Afghanistan border late Sunday, followed by strikes against militant hideouts and safe havens, killing 29 fighters, Pakistan's Information Minister Attaullah Tarar said. Pakistan said the operations were launched in response to multiple militant attacks across Pakistan.

item.title: Pakistan forces kill 29 militants in border operation, Kabul says dozens of civilians dead
item.description: By Mohammad Yunus Yawar KABUL, June 29 (Reuters) - Pakistan's security forces killed at least 29 militants in ground and air operations along the Afghanistan border, it said on Monday, while the

item.title: North Korea commissions 5,000-ton destroyer Choe Hyon, plans 10,000-ton nuclear cruisers
item.description: North Korea commissioned the Choe Hyon (51) first-in-class destroyer — with Kim Jong Un announcing plans for two annual surface ship builds.

item.title: Pakistan strikes on eastern Afghanistan kill dozens
item.description: Pakistani airstrikes killed dozens of people in eastern Afghanistan, with Islamabad saying on Monday it targeted militants but the Afghan government reporting civilian casualties. The Taliban government said the airstrikes hit three eastern provinces, killing 36 civilians and wounding 163.

item.description: Timir Ahmed Mohamed is accused of the attempted murder of five people in Ealing, west London.

item.title: Scotland's papers: Scotland squad fly home and Scottish Labour indy review
item.description: A review of the front page stories from the daily newspapers in Scotland.

item.title: Europe Today: Trump's tariff threat, Iran talks and Europe's heatwave
item.description: The US and Iran are set to resume talks in Qatar after a tense weekend of renewed threats, while Europe battles a deadly heatwave linked to more than 1,300 deaths. Attention also turns to growing EU-US trade tensions and the debate over how Europe should adapt to extreme temperatures.

item.title: Russian high schools now require 17 hours of basic military training classes for 6th to 11th grade
item.description: Russia is doubling the amount of military training time in high schools, with new lessons on drones and field exercises.
